mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate at 0.08% (near zero lower bound) combined with month-over-month CPI at 235.64 signals an accommodative monetary regime. Sustained low rates compress long-term discount rates, causing 10-year Treasury yields to decline as investors seek duration. This mechanism operates continuously in low-rate environments.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.08% indicates near-zero policy rate
regime_hint: monetary accommodation at zero lower bound
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises more than 15 basis points over 20 trading days despite FEDFUNDS remaining below 0.25%, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any rolling 2-year window in low-rate regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate at 6.7% combined with payroll employment at 137,928 thousand indicates labor market softening. When unemployment rises above 6.5% while payroll growth decelerates, equity risk premium expands and investors rotate into safe-haven Treasuries, driving 10-year yields lower as demand for duration increases.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 6.7% signals labor market weakness
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises more than 10 basis points over 20 trading days in 3 or more consecutive triggered instances, or joint signal (UNRATE > 6.5% AND PAYEMS growth < 100k) fails to predict negative equity returns in > 50% of occurrences.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales at 433,907 million dollars show consumer spending resilience. When retail sales growth remains positive and accelerates month-over-month, it signals robust aggregate demand that supports economic growth expectations, causing 10-year Treasury yields to rise as growth premium widens and inflation expectations increase.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 433,907M indicates sustained consumer demand
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield declines more than 10 basis points over 5 trading days despite RSAFS growth > 0.5%, or positive RSAFS growth fails to predict positive 5-day equity returns in > 55% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production at 101.63 (index 2017=100) combined with housing starts at 907 thousand indicates supply-side capacity constraints emerging. When INDPRO growth decelerates below 0.2% month-over-month while HOUST remains elevated, supply bottlenecks drive inflation expectations higher, causing 10-year Treasury yields to rise as real rates compress.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 101.63 near trend, HOUST at 907k elevated
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield declines more than 20 basis points over 60 trading days in 2 or more consecutive triggered instances, or interaction signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns in > 52% of occurrences.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts at 907 thousand combined with real GDP growth trajectory (GDPC1 at 15,942.3B) indicate construction-led demand. When housing starts exceed 900k units, forward-looking construction activity signals sustained capital expenditure and employment growth, causing 10-year Treasury yields to rise as growth expectations strengthen.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 907k indicates strong housing demand
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield declines more than 25 basis points over 60 trading days when HOUST > 900k for 2+ consecutive months, or positive housing starts fail to predict positive 60-day equity returns in > 53% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: CPI at 235.64 combined with FEDFUNDS at 0.08% creates a deeply negative real policy rate. When real rates remain below -2% (nominal rate minus inflation), central bank policy is maximally accommodative. This regime sustains equity valuations through low discount rates but eventually triggers inflation expectations, causing 10-year Treasury yields to rise as markets price future rate normalization.
evidence_refs: fact:Real policy rate deeply negative at current levels
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield declines more than 15 basis points over 20 trading days in 3+ consecutive triggered instances, or negative real rate signal fails to predict positive 20-day returns in > 54% of occurrences.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment at 137,928 thousand with unemployment at 6.7% indicates labor market slack. When unemployment rises above 6.5% while payroll growth remains positive but decelerating (< 50k month-over-month), the labor market enters a transition phase where growth expectations weaken, causing 10-year Treasury yields to decline as investors reduce growth premium.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE > 6.5% and PAYEMS month-over-month growth between 0k and 50k
evidence_refs: fact:Labor market showing mixed signals with elevated unemployment
regime_hint: labor market transition from expansion to slowdown
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises more than 12 basis points over 5 trading days in 2+ consecutive triggered instances, or transition signal fails to predict negative 5-day returns in > 51% of occurrences.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production at 101.63 indicates manufacturing near trend. When INDPRO growth turns negative month-over-month (declining from prior month), it signals demand weakness and potential recession risk. This supply-side deterioration causes equity risk premium to expand and investors to seek duration, driving 10-year Treasury yields lower as safe-haven demand increases.
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises more than 15 basis points over 20 trading days when INDPRO declines month-over-month, or negative INDPRO growth fails to predict negative 20-day equity returns in > 55% of instances.
